title: Adapter Api Actor Resolver
description: HTTP handlers record `by` on history events through `ActorResolver`, but authenticated deployments will attach an `ApiActor` per request. This adapter is the bridge: with `disabled` it forwards to the kernel resolver; with a future auth type it maps `ApiActor` into `ActorIdentity` deterministically.
context: HTTP handlers record `by` on history events through `ActorResolver`, but authenticated deployments will attach an `ApiActor` per request. This adapter is the bridge: with `disabled` it forwards to the kernel resolver; with a future auth type it maps `ApiActor` into `ActorIdentity` deterministically.

rules.requirement: disabled auth delegates to the kernel ActorResolver
rules: When effective `api.auth.type` is `disabled`, the adapter MUST implement `ActorResolver` by forwarding `resolve()` to the `ActorResolver` instance supplied at server bootstrap. It MUST NOT read `Authorization` headers and MUST NOT fabricate identity from HTTP metadata.

rules.requirement: authenticated requests map ApiActor to ActorIdentity
rules: When request context carries an `ApiActor` from a registered verifier (future auth types), the adapter MUST map `id`, `name`, `email`, and optional `roles` into the `ActorIdentity` fields used by change history. The mapping MUST be deterministic for a given `ApiActor` payload.

rules.requirement: one resolved actor per HTTP request
rules: The adapter MUST resolve actor at most once per request and return the same identity for every mutating kernel call in that request.

constraints: HTTP handlers MUST NOT import @specd/core from @specd/ui or @specd/client.
v1 server auth: api.auth.type from specd.yaml (never studio.*); registry registers only disabled; no server-side Bearer enforcement on loopback or specd ui serve.
Artifact save/load MUST use core:save-change-artifact and core:get-change-artifact — not raw ChangeRepository.saveArtifact from HTTP handlers.
There is no GET /changes/{name}/validation resource; use GET .../status and POST .../validate.
Canonical workspace spec artifacts are read-only in Studio v1.

scenarios.requirement: disabled auth delegates to the kernel ActorResolver
scenarios.name: Disabled auth forwards to bootstrap ActorResolver
when: HTTP handler resolves actor for a mutating request
then: adapter resolve() delegates to the kernel ActorResolver
AND no Authorization header is read

scenarios.requirement: disabled auth delegates to the kernel ActorResolver
scenarios.name: Git-configured actor is used for history
given: git user.name and user.email are configured
when: SaveChangeArtifact records history on the request
then: AND no synthetic API actor is created

scenarios.requirement: disabled auth delegates to the kernel ActorResolver
scenarios.name: Same actor for multiple mutations in one request
given: one HTTP request performs two kernel writes
when: actor is resolved twice via the adapter
then: both resolutions return the same identity

scenarios.requirement: authenticated requests map ApiActor to ActorIdentity
scenarios.name: ApiActor maps to history fields
given: request context carries ApiActor { id, name, email }
when: adapter resolves actor for a mutating call
then: ActorIdentity uses the same name and email
AND mapping is stable for identical input

scenarios.requirement: authenticated requests map ApiActor to ActorIdentity
scenarios.name: Optional roles are preserved when present
then: roles are available to future authorization checks

scenarios.requirement: authenticated requests map ApiActor to ActorIdentity
scenarios.name: Different ApiActor yields different identity
given: two requests with different ApiActor payloads
then: AND adapter does not cache across requests

scenarios.requirement: one resolved actor per HTTP request
scenarios.name: First resolve is cached for the request
when: adapter resolve() is called twice in the same request scope
then: both calls return the same object identity or equal fields

scenarios.requirement: one resolved actor per HTTP request
scenarios.name: New request gets fresh resolution
when: each request resolves actor at handler entry
then: AND no leakage of actor between requests

scenarios.requirement: one resolved actor per HTTP request
scenarios.name: Concurrent requests do not share actor cache
then: each resolution uses its own request context
AND identities do not cross streams
